Outside of volunteering, I enjoy reading, writing, and photography. Poetry is something I especially connect with because it allows me to express emotions.
interestReason: I am interested in joining Science Olympiad because I want to challenge myself and grow academically. Last year, I took the diagnostic and realized that I was severely unprepared. So this year, I want to truly dedicate myself to studying and preparing so that I can improve and show what I’m capable of.

bio: Hi! My name is Tanisa and I'm a rising junior. Outside of SciOly, I'm doing research and working on getting my photography and writing published in various magazines. In my free time, I love baking, writing, and volunteering. I love cats and I have two of my own!
pastExperience: I have been in entomology, microbe mission, material science, and experimental design. 5th place in microbe mission in LexSO.
returnReason: I want to continue being part of the SciOly community, especially after seeing how hardworking and passionate everyone is. The encouragement that everyone receives within the team is also super inspiring.

bio: My name is Ryan Yun, and I am a rising senior. I am interested in a variety of sports, video games, and napping. I am interested in studying something related to environmental science in college, and I look forward to any opportunity to learn more about the STEM field.
pastExperience: I have been on the Bronx Science Science Olympiad team for the past two years (sophomore and junior year). Although I haven't been able to compete as much, I attended regular meetings for events such as Fossils, Experimental Design, and Geological Mapping.
returnReason: I was selected to be an Event Manager for Rocks and Minerals this year, which is super exciting, and I feel as if this is the best way for me to meaningfully contribute to the team and help this team grow and compete. Creating binder pages for my event has allowed me to learn and develop new learning strategies that would help team members succeed in my respective event, and I can't wait to start the school year and fill in that teaching role and meet new members. I also want to learn more about science, and Science Olympiad has taught me more about the general sciences beyond the classroom these past two years.

bio: I'm Sophia and I'm currently a rising junior. I love exploring and researching different fields of science, but I'm particularly passionate about biology. Outside of Science Olympiad I enjoy volunteering, going shopping and hanging out with my friends, dance and always finding new cdramas or kdramas to watch. I also love mangos and my favorite dessert place is MangoMango!
pastExperience: '24-'25 Science Olympiad Member
returnReason: I would like to participate in Science Olympiad again this year because last year I really enjoyed the environment and community that SciOly fostered among its members. It provided a good learning environment for everyone and I was able to learn a lot from the weekly lessons and activities provided by the EMs. SciOly gave me the opportunity to explore more areas of science that aren't regularly included in school courses. Additionally, through SciOly I was able to meet a lot of new people that also were also passionate about science, specifically biology, and I hope to meet more this year.

bio: I like to play tennis, I am a junior, and I am interested in science.

Also, I think that SciOly can help me develop critical thinking skills as well as STEM skills that I may find useful later in college.

bio: I love reading different books, especially thriller and mysteries that challenge my mind. I set a reading goal every year too! 50 for 2025 and I’ve met it already. Not only that, I also love anatomy, epidemiology, psychology, and toxicology. I find these topics so fascinating and I love researching about them. For example, my favorite poisonous plant is belladonna. I used to flip through biology textbooks and read about the different body systems and names of different organs in elementary.
interestReason: I love science and working with others to build something! Overall, I want to experience team work under a competitive environment. This way, I can increase stress tolerance and my collaboration skills even more, which can be beneficial skills in general.
